key_departures.impact: Led the Cowboys in sacks (8.5) and pressures (40) with 12 TFL after joining mid-season on a 1-year deal. Cowboys' tone changed after hiring DC Christian Parker, who favors a different EDGE profile. Departure leaves a significant pass-rush void.

7-year Cowboy and long-tenured starter was slowed by injuries in 2025 and deemed unlikely to return under Parker's new scheme. Departure created a starting safety vacancy that the team quickly moved to fill.

Departed in 2025 free agency (prior cycle) to Seattle, where he won Super Bowl LX. Generated two compensatory fifth-round picks for Dallas in the 2026 draft. Already gone from the roster but officially recognized for compensatory purposes this cycle.
Had only 3 sacks and 40 pressures after posting 10.5 sacks with Washington in 2024. Door closed on return. Further thins an already depleted EDGE room.
Departed in 2025 free agency (prior cycle) to Jacksonville. Was the team's primary nickel CB. Departure recognized for compensatory pick purposes this cycle.

Acquired at 2025 trade deadline from Bengals. Posted a 52.4 PFF grade in 7 appearances; did not provide expected spark. Released February 2, 2026, freeing $6.5M cap space. Worsened an already thin LB room — DeMarvion Overshown now the only returning starter.
Was a liability in 2025; Cowboys moved on. Departure further deepens the LB need.
Suffered a rib injury in preseason, stashed on IR, did not play in 2025. UFA departure is roster cleanup.
RFA not tendered. Interior OL depth loss. Cowboys retain TJ Bass on 2nd-round tender as primary IOL backup.

contract.notes: Existing Packers contract (4yr/$96M signed 2023) inherited via trade. $18M base salary in 2026, $21M in 2027. No guaranteed money remaining. Cowboys paid a 2027 4th-round pick to acquire. Restructure expected to lower 2026 cap number. AAV reflects 2026 cap charge per OTC.

signings.analysis: Pro Bowl EDGE in 2024 with 46.5 career sacks. Posted 7.5 sacks in 2025 but production fell off sharply in the second half. Acquired for a 2027 4th-round pick — low trade cost for a proven pass rusher with DC familiarity. However, zero guaranteed money remaining signals a bridge arrangement, not a long-term cornerstone. Upgrades the room meaningfully as a primary starter opposite Donovan Ezeiruaku (who is injured through OTAs). EDGE remains a need — Dallas still lacks depth and continuity at the position, and the draft remains the likely path to a long-term solution.

contract.notes: 3yr/$33M base with max value up to $36M with incentives. $9.7M signing bonus. $22M guaranteed per Spotrac. Per ESPN, contract includes cap-friendly structure in 2026.

signings.analysis: Five-year starter with 87 career starts for Arizona, 9 INTs and 578 career tackles. Played alongside Budda Baker. Reunites with new Cowboys secondary coach Ryan Smith from Arizona. Three-year deal with $22M guaranteed signals legitimate starter investment, not a bridge move. Pairs with restructured Malik Hooker (retained) to give Dallas two capable starting safeties plus P.J. Locke as third option. S need goes from critical to largely resolved. A safety may still be targeted in mid-rounds of the draft for depth/developmental purposes, but no longer a premium priority.

contract.notes: 1yr/$5M per Ian Rapoport/NFL Network. Spotrac lists $2.5M guaranteed. Reunites with DC Christian Parker who coached him in Denver.

signings.analysis: One-year prove-it deal at depth money. Was the third safety in Denver in 2025 after spine surgery, made three regular season starts but started both playoff games when Brandon Jones went on IR. Strong run defender and box safety candidate for Parker's scheme. Gives Dallas three starting-caliber safeties and schematic flexibility to move Thompson into nickel/rover roles. Adds legitimate depth at a position the Cowboys just filled with Thompson. Low-risk, scheme-specific add.

contract.notes: 1yr max $3M per ESPN's Todd Archer. Cap hit $3.95M per Archer. Guaranteed portion includes base salary guarantees. ERFA-level cost structure.

signings.analysis: Former 2nd-round pick returning on a 1-year, low-cost deal. Coming off torn ACL — posted 1 sack, 25 pressures and 7 TFL in 17 games (5 starts) in 2025. Had 8.5 sacks in first two years before injury; Parker's 3-4 OLB scheme could unlock more production. However, 1-year structure and below-starter money clearly signal depth/rotational role, not a solution. Minimally addresses EDGE depth — need remains. Cowboys still expect to address EDGE in the draft.

contract.notes: 1yr/$3M per Mike Garafolo/NFL Network. Guarantees estimated based on market for 1-year depth deals.

signings.analysis: True nose tackle (6'4, 320 lbs) at age 25, finishing his rookie contract with LAC. 20 starts in 4 years; played 25% snap share in 2025 after elbow injury. Slots into a very crowded IDL rotation (Quinnen Williams, Kenny Clark, Osa Odighizuwa, Solomon Thomas, Jay Toia). Comes from Jim Harbaugh's 3-4 system, directly analogous to Parker's new scheme. Depth/rotational add at a position of no significant need. Adds nose tackle mass and special teams value. IDL was not a pre-FA need; this signing does not move the needle there.

contract.notes: 3yr/$24M, $16M fully guaranteed per OTC/Spotrac. Includes $6M signing bonus plus guaranteed roster bonuses and salary in 2026 and 2027. Cap hit just $3.95M in 2026 due to void years. Easy out in 2028.

signings.analysis: Re-signed before free agency opened (deal agreed ~March 1). Williams had a career-best 2025: 1,201 rushing yards, 11 TDs on 252 carries, ranked 4th in YAC/carry. Deal is richly structured but cap-friendly in 2026 ($3.95M hit). Tied for 16th among RBs in AAV ($8M) — fair value for a 1,200-yard back. RB was not a pre-FA need and retaining Williams locks it down through at least 2027. No change to RB need tier.

contract.notes: Non-exclusive franchise tag. Tag value $27,298,000 per NFL/Spotrac. Sides have until July 15 to negotiate long-term extension. Long-term deal likely deferred to post-2026 season per reporting. Tag is fully guaranteed.

signings.analysis: Tag secures Pickens through 2026. Career-best 2025: 93 catches, 1,429 yards, 9 TDs alongside CeeDee Lamb. Non-exclusive tag means other teams can negotiate — Cowboys have 5-day right-of-first-refusal or 2 first-round picks as compensation. Long-term extension talks likely to continue through summer, but WR is not a draft need with both Pickens and Lamb locked in for 2026. No WR draft need created.

contract.notes: Existing deal restructured on March 9 per Todd Archer, converting $11M roster bonus to spread the cap hit, freeing ~$2M. Now effectively 1yr/$5M structure. Retained, not a free agent signing.

signings.analysis: Cowboys restructured Hooker's deal to retain him and create cap room. In five seasons: 289 tackles, 14 PDs, 7 INTs. Cap-friendly restructure. Contributes to filling the S need alongside Thompson and Locke. Counted here as a retained/restructured player; the need at S is addressed primarily by the Thompson signing.

contract.notes: 2nd-round restricted free agent tender at $5.76M. Fully guaranteed for 2026. Long-term extension talks expected to continue.

signings.analysis: All-Pro kicker retained on RFA tender while long-term deal is negotiated. Cowboys held right of first refusal. K was not a need; tender simply maintains the status quo.

contract.notes: 2nd-round restricted free agent tender at $5.76M per team announcement.

signings.analysis: Versatile IOL backup retained on RFA tender — appeared in 31 games with 8 starts across multiple OL positions in 2025. With Brock Hoffman not tendered and departing, Bass is now the primary IOL backup. IOL is a moderate need but Bass retention plus existing starters (Tyler Smith, Zack Martin era over, current starters in place) prevents escalation.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Thompson (3yr/$33M, $22M gtd) is a legitimate starter investment paired with retained Malik Hooker and depth signing P.J. Locke. Dallas now has three starting-caliber safeties. Safety drops from a real need to a luxury/late-round consideration. No premium draft pick warranted here.

Gary (trade, existing contract, no guarantees remaining) is a starting-caliber addition but structured as a bridge. Sam Williams is a depth/rotational re-sign on a 1-year deal. Ezeiruaku (2025 2nd-rounder) is out through OTAs with hip surgery. James Houston and Marist Liufau (LB-to-OLB convert) round out a thin room. EDGE remains a high priority for the draft — likely one of Dallas's two first-round picks (12th or 20th overall) goes here. Drops from tier 1 critical to tier 2 high priority because a starter is now in place, but not a long-term answer.

Logan Wilson released, Kenneth Murray departed, Nakobe Dean and Quay Walker both signed with the Raiders (Cowboys missed out on both). DeMarvion Overshown is the only returning starting LB. Shemar James (rookie) played on necessity. Marist Liufau converting to OLB further depletes the room. LB is now the most critical unaddressed need — escalated to tier 1. Must be addressed in the draft and/or remaining free agency.

No CB was signed in the first wave of free agency. Jourdan Lewis (nickel) is gone. Reddy Steward (ERFA tenderd, consistent in 2025) and Josh Butler (ERFA tendered) are retained minimums. DaRon Bland is under contract but coming off injury. CB remains a tier 2 need; the nickel position is a particular concern under Parker's scheme. Draft or later free agency spending required.
Javonte Williams (3yr/$24M, $16M gtd) re-signed before free agency on a strong multi-year deal after a 1,201-yard, 11-TD season. RB need eliminated. No draft capital needed at this position.

George Pickens retained on franchise tag ($27.3M, fully guaranteed for 2026) after 93/1,429/9 TDs season. CeeDee Lamb under long-term deal. WR is a strength, not a need. Long-term Pickens extension talks ongoing but his presence in 2026 is secured.

Cowboys already had Quinnen Williams, Kenny Clark, Osa Odighizuwa, and Solomon Thomas. Addition of Otito Ogbonnia as a nose tackle adds depth. IDL is now one of the deepest units on the roster. A pass-rushing IDL/DT could still be an attractive pick in day 2-3 of the draft, but no longer a need.

key_additions.contract_summary: Trade (existing 2yr/$42M, $0 gtd remaining); 2027 4th-round pick to GB
key_additions.headline: Pro Bowl pass rusher (46.5 career sacks) acquired via trade to anchor depleted EDGE room under DC Christian Parker, his former Packers coach
Five-year Cardinals starter (87 starts) fills critical safety void left by Donovan Wilson departure on a genuine multi-year investment
Cowboys lock up 1,201-yard, 11-TD breakout back before free agency market, keeping offensive engine in place at fair value
Star WR (93/1,429/9 TDs in 2025) retained on franchise tag while Cowboys pursue long-term extension; forms elite WR tandem with CeeDee Lamb
DC Parker reunites with former Broncos safety for depth/nickel versatility, giving Dallas three starting-caliber safeties entering 2026
Former 2nd-round pick retained on low-cost deal as rotational EDGE in Parker's 3-4 OLB scheme, reuniting with the system that could revive his career
25-year-old nose tackle (6'4/320) adds run-stuffing bulk to an already-deep IDL rotation from a familiar Harbaugh 3-4 system

key_losses.headline: Team's sack leader (8.5 sacks, 40 pressures in 2025) departs as UFA after scheme change under new DC Parker, leaving the biggest hole on the roster
7-year Cowboy and 75-start safety let go as Parker revamps secondary; departure directly triggered the Thompson and Locke signings
Trade-deadline acquisition posted a 52.4 PFF grade and was released, leaving the Cowboys with only one returning starting LB and a massive void at the position
Former 10.5-sack rusher failed to replicate Washington production in Dallas (3 sacks), further thinning a depleted EDGE room
Backup center/guard depth departs as Cowboys prioritize other positions; T.J. Bass (tendered) remains as primary IOL reserve
